description_long: This is a classic A-frame tent with a muted olive green fabric exterior, supported by a central ridge and anchored by six guy lines extending from each corner and midpoint. The tent features a triangular entrance at one end, which is open and reveals a dark interior, while the opposite end is fully closed. The fabric appears to be a durable, weather-resistant canvas or synthetic material, and the tent is held taut by ropes staked into the ground. The structure is simple, with no visible windows or additional compartments, and the roof slopes steeply to shed rain. The tent is compact and utilitarian, suitable for temporary shelter in parks, urban green spaces, or emergency scenarios.
description: A green A-frame tent with a triangular entrance, supported by guy lines and stakes, made of weather-resistant fabric. The tent is compact, simple, and designed for temporary outdoor shelter.
description_view_0: Front view showing the closed triangular end of the tent with guy lines extending outward.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the rectangular side panel and the symmetrical arrangement of guy lines.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the open triangular entrance and visible dark interior.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the other rectangular side panel and guy lines.
attribute_entrance_type: Triangular flap entrance located at one end of the tent, open and without visible zippers or mesh.
attribute_guy_line_configuration: Six guy lines, attached at each corner and midpoint of the tent, staked outward in a symmetrical pattern.
